tool_name: Crane Runway Beam — Maximum Wheel Load, Vertical & Lateral Bending, Fatigue Check

inputs.name: Crane Rated Lifting Capacity (W_rated)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Crane Bridge (Girder) Self-Weight (W_bridge)
inputs.note: From crane manufacturer data sheet; rough estimate: W_bridge ≈ 0.3–0.5 × W_rated for standard EOT cranes
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Crane Trolley & Hoist Self-Weight (W_trolley)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Crane Service Class (CMAA / FEM 1.001)

options.label: CMAA Class C — Light service (2–5 lifts/hr)
CMAA Class F — Severe continuous duty

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Runway Beam Section Modulus — Vertical (I_x)
inputs.note: From standard beam tables: W24×55 → I_x = 1350 cm⁴; W36×135 → I_x = 7800 cm⁴
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: N_wheels_per_rail = 2; P_static_kn = (crane_capacity_kn + trolley_weight_kn + bridge_weight_kn / 2) / N_wheels_per_rail   // [kN] Maximum Static Wheel Load (P_wheel) Reference: CMAA 70 §3.3 — max wheel load when trolley at end of bridge; symmetric 2-wheel per rail assumed

phi_v = {A:1.10, C:1.15, D:1.20, E:1.25, F:1.30}[crane_class]; P_factored_kn = P_static_kn * phi_v   // [dimensionless, kN] Dynamic Impact Factor (Φ_v) & Factored Wheel Load Reference: AISC Design Guide 7 Table 2.1 / CMAA 70 — vertical dynamic impact factor by crane class

beam_self_wt_kn_m = 0.8; M_max_knm = P_factored_kn * runway_beam_span_m / 4 + beam_self_wt_kn_m * Math.pow(runway_beam_span_m, 2) / 8   // [kN·m] Maximum Vertical Bending Moment in Runway Beam (M_max) Reference: Midspan moment: moving concentrated load at ¼-span produces near-maximum moment; + UDL self-weight

S_x_cm3 = 2 * Ix_beam_cm4 / beam_depth_mm * 10; sigma_bend_mpa = M_max_knm * 1e6 / (S_x_cm3 * 1e3)   // [MPa] Extreme Fibre Bending Stress (σ_bend) Reference: σ = M/S_x; S_x = 2I_x/d — elastic section modulus

H_lat_kn = 0.20 * (crane_capacity_kn + trolley_weight_kn)   // [kN] Lateral (Horizontal) Crane Thrust Force (H_lat) Reference: AISC DG7 §3.2 / CMAA 70 §3.5 — lateral thrust H = 20% of (lifted load + trolley) for electric overhead cranes

fatigue_cat = {A:'E (120 MPa)', C:'E (110 MPa)', D:'D (110 MPa)', E:'C (165 MPa, check)', F:'B (165 MPa, check)'}[crane_class]; stress_range_mpa = sigma_bend_mpa * 0.6   // [text, MPa] Fatigue Life Category & Allowable Stress Range Reference: AISC DG7 §5 / ASCE 7-22 Table 15.4-2 — runway beam fatigue category per crane class; stress range = σ_max × 0.6 (trolley at end)

V1.error_msg: Bending stress > 165 MPa: exceeds allowable for A36/S275 steel (0.66Fy) — upsize beam section or reduce span

V2.error_msg: L/d ratio > 25: shallow beam relative to span — deflection likely excessive; AISC DG7 recommends L/1000 vertical limit; check I_x

V3.error_msg: Fatigue stress range exceeds AISC Category D allowable (110 MPa) — weld details and connection design critical; consider Category C details

smart_warnings.message: Class E/F crane: heavy fatigue regime — runway beam fatigue life governs over static strength; use AISC Category B/C weld details and specify weld quality per AWS D1.1
High lateral thrust: verify top flange bracing and rail clip design — AISC DG7 §4 requires continuous top flange bracing for heavy crane classes
Long bridge span + moderate duty: trolley skewing forces become significant — verify bumper and end stop design per CMAA 70 §3.6
High bending stress: verify lateral-torsional buckling of top compression flange per AISC 360-22 F2 — crane beams often unbraced between columns
Wheel load > 500 kN: heavy crane — verify column base plate, anchor bolts, and foundation design for combined gravity and lateral crane thrust per AISC DG1

fmea.effect: Bending stress > 165 MPa: exceeds allowable for A36/S275 steel (0.66Fy) — upsize beam section or reduce span
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: L/d ratio > 25: shallow beam relative to span — deflection likely excessive; AISC DG7 recommends L/1000 vertical limit; check I_x
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Fatigue stress range exceeds AISC Category D allowable (110 MPa) — weld details and connection design critical; consider Category C details
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Class E/F crane: heavy fatigue regime — runway beam fatigue life governs over static strength; use AISC Category B/C weld details and specify weld quality per AWS D1.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: crane_class === 'E' || crane_class === 'F'

fmea.failureMode: High lateral thrust: verify top flange bracing and rail clip design — AISC DG7 §4 requires continuous top flange bracing for heavy crane classes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: H_lat_kn > crane_capacity_kn * 0.25

fmea.failureMode: Long bridge span + moderate duty: trolley skewing forces become significant — verify bumper and end stop design per CMAA 70 §3.6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bridge_span_m > 20 && crane_class === 'D'

fmea.failureMode: High bending stress: verify lateral-torsional buckling of top compression flange per AISC 360-22 F2 — crane beams often unbraced between columns
Wheel load > 500 kN: heavy crane — verify column base plate, anchor bolts, and foundation design for combined gravity and lateral crane thrust per AISC DG1
